text: Hello everyone. I want to talk about why I built Sky for a Bridge and some of the technical
challenges behind it. My name is Ryo. I'm from Japan. This is my first time speaking at
an international conference, so I'm a bit nervous. But I'm very happy to be here and
very grateful to Blue Sky for the support. So I work very hard to memorize the first part.
But after this, I'll leave from my home. Please give me a - let me start. I learned
this tool because of my personal experience. When Blue Sky first appeared, I was really
excited. A new social network was starting and it was open. I thought that was very interesting,
so I made an account early. But when I started using it, it felt a little lonely. Why? Because I did not know many people there.
Making account was easy. But when I looked at my timeline, the people I usually follow were not there.
My friends, my co-workers and people like to leave where are missing.
Without familiar people, a social network can feel very bright.
So I started doing this by hand. I think many people do the same.
I open the blue sky search, type the name or username or someone I knew from X, and if I found them, I follow them.
Then I searched for the next person and the next person. But if it was hard, it took a lot of time. It was trying, tiring.
And some time, I still could not find the right person because the name was a little different or they use a different account.
Then I thought, this is not just my problem. A lot of people moving to blue sky, we are doing the same thing.
So I thought, it should be easier.
But I thought, this is not just my problem. A lot of people moving to blue sky, we are doing the same thing.
So I thought, it should be easier. That is why I build Sky for a bridge.
This tool reads the people you follow on platform like X, looks for matching blue sky account and helps you follow them more easily.
It helps with task people we are doing one by one by hand.
First, I think it is easy to understand with the demo. So let me show you one.
Open your profile on X.
Click the Sky for a bridge extension.
Enter your handle and log in.
Open your following list.
Click the extension again.
Let's find Blue Sky users.
Scanning your following list.
Detecting Blue Sky users.
OK, let's check results.
About 500 users spawned.
On the left, X users.
On the right, much Blue Sky users.
Oh, thank you.
Let's follow one.
Check it on Blue Sky.
Follows successfully.

Unfollowed.
Unfollowed.

Unfollowed successfully.
And so that's how easy it is to follow users.
Thank you.
That's the end of the term.
There was a useful future, too.
So please try, if you're interested, you can use it on Chrome and Firefox.
Next, I want to talk about two technical challenges.
The first challenge was how to get the list of people of user followers.
How do we get the list of people of user followers?
At first, I thought I would use the API, but then X changed that a lot.
As many of you remember, API access became very expensive for an individual developer
like me.
That was not realistic.
So I changed my approach.
Instead of using the API, I use the browser page.
The user was looking at.
The user was already logged in, and the falling list was already on the screen.
So I viewed our browser extension.
It reads the information directly from the DOM.
That becomes a starting point, this tool.
So it also gives me a clear structure.
Only the first part depends on each platform.
That part is reading the page.
After that, the rest can be shared.
Searching for blue sky accounts, showing the result, following account, and running search.
And because of that, it became much easier to support my platform.
Right now, X, Facebook, TikTok, Instagram, and threads.
Of course, every platform has different DOM, so it is not easy.
But this design made it much easier to add support for more platform.
The second challenge was matching accuracy.
How do you find the right person?
This part was very important.
People do not always use the same name across platforms.
Some things the name is similar, and some time is complex difference.
So find the right blue sky account is not easy.
I use a few methods together.
The basic one are the display name and account name.
I also use the profile text to become some people put their blue sky account in the X profile.
Recently, I added the avatar image matching.
Many people use some profile image across platforms.
So I use that as a hint.
This works better than I expected.
Over time, the project also became much bigger than I expected.
Now, people from many countries use it, and it has been downloaded more than 520,000.
Thank you.
Thank you.
When I started this project, I never thought so many people would use it.
That is still surprising to me.
And I was very happy when the official blue sky account shared it,
and when users told me it helps them.
What I learned is simple.
When people move to new social network, making an account is easy.
Finding people again is a hard part.
And when we have to help with that, moving become much easier.
So I want to leave you with one message.
Open protocol needs migration, too.
Open technology is important.
But if we want people to really move, we also need bridges.
Skyflower Bridge is one of those bridges, and I hope we will see many more in the future.
Oh, thank you.
